body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:model-select

route: driver-gap (queued tooling work, NOT frozen)

## Reasoning (re-derived from scratch â€” opencode v1.14.50, current driver)

### Agent supports per-turn model switching (yes)

opencode v1.14.50 (verified `opencode --version`) exposes mid-session model switching three ways, all confirmed against the live binary's `$bunfs` strings:

- **`/models` slash command** â€” "Use /models to see and switch between available AI models". It opens the `DialogSelectModel` popover, NOT a one-shot switch.
- **`<leader>m` keybind** (leader = `ctrl+x`, i.e. `ctrl+x m`) â€” the `model.list` command, same `DialogSelectModel` overlay.
- **`f2` / `shift+f2`** â€” `model.cycle_recent` / `model.cycle_recent_reverse`: cycle directly to a recent model with no overlay (depends on prior selection history, so non-deterministic for a fresh session). `model.cycle_favorite` is bound to `none` by default.

Headless `opencode run` also takes **`-m, --model <provider/model>`** (confirmed in `opencode run --help`). So the agent CAN run turn 1 on model A and a later turn on model B within one `--session` chain. `capabilities.json` declares `multi_model_orchestration: true`. This is the per-turn-model primitive the scenario exercises. Verdict: yes.

### Daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state straight from SQLite, never tailing JSONL. `ComputeMetrics` (`metrics.go`) reads `message.data.model.modelID` per message and on every non-empty value sets `metrics.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(modelID)` â€” i.e. latest-non-empty-wins, exactly the Aâ†’B promotion the scenario asserts. Token totals accumulate via `ev.Tokens.Total` across messages regardless of which model produced them. A genuine per-turn switch (turn 2's assistant message carrying a different `model.modelID`) WOULD therefore be observed: ModelName follows to model B, TotalTokens grows. The daemon is not the blocker.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: model-select)

The scenario needs ONE session to run model A on turn 1 and model B on turn 2 with the change observed per turn. The opencode driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh`) now has TWO paths, and NEITHER can switch the model mid-session:

1. **Headless path** (`run_send`, the default route) implements only `send | wait_turn | sleep`, and the `send` arm hardcodes `opencode run --format default [--session <id>] -- "$text"` with NO `--model` flag â€” so every turn uses the single config default (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` â†’ `lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`). No primitive selects a model per send.
2. **Live-TUI path** (`run_live`, routed when a step has type `reset_session` or `slash`; drives the TUI under tmux via `tmux send-keys`) implements `send | wait_turn | reset_session | slash | sleep`. Its `slash` arm types a literal command + `Enter` â€” it can OPEN `/models`, but `/models` is the `DialogSelectModel` arrow-key overlay, and the live path has NO `keys` step to send Up/Down + Enter to NAVIGATE the picker and select model B. (`f2`/`model.cycle_recent` is likewise undrivable: no `keys` step, and it depends on a non-deterministic recent-model history.)

So across both paths the driver can open the picker at best, never complete the selection, and the headless path that DOES reach a turn can't target a second model. The missing capability is per-step model selection, not a recipe-lint step-TYPE gap.

### Ruled out: inline `/model` is not a send-only escape hatch

The prior pass (2026-05-26) probed this live: a `send`-path run of `opencode run -- "/model ionos/meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct"` produced a session whose only message still carried `model.modelID = gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored` / `providerID = lmstudio` â€” the `/model` text went to the LLM as content; the model did not switch. Consistent with the binary surface: `/models` is a TUI dialog opener, not a headless-`run`-processed inline directive. There is no send-only way to reach a second model.

## What would unblock it â€” name the primitive

**Missing primitive: per-step model selection in the driver (gap:model-select).** The cleanest, most deterministic fix is to thread the headless flag, not to drive the arrow-key overlay:

- Add an optional `model` field to the `send` step (or a dedicated `model-select` step) that threads `opencode run -m <provider/model>` in `run_send`. This is fully deterministic and needs no TUI navigation.
- Alternatively, add a `keys` step to the live-TUI path so a recipe can open `/models` (or `ctrl+x m`) and arrow-navigate `DialogSelectModel` â€” but this is more fragile and the headless `-m` route is preferred.
- Model A = config default `lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`; model B = a second reachable model (e.g. `ionos/meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct`). IONOS models require `IONOS_API_TOKEN`, which is exported in `~/.zshrc` (so present in the recording shell) â€” confirm it resolves at record time, or add a second `lmstudio` model (only one is declared today) as the model-B fallback.

Once the driver runs turn 1 on model A and turn 2 on model B in one `--session` chain, the cell records: the daemon already reads per-turn model from the store, so summary.model_name follows latest-wins in the .replay.json.golden. `expected.jsonl` is retained as the two-turn lifecycle skeleton (the events.jsonl phase DSL has no model field).

## Confidence

0.85: the driver grammar (both paths, no `keys`/no `--model` in send) is read directly from the live driver source; the TUI mechanism (`/models`â†’`DialogSelectModel`, `<leader>m`=`ctrl+x m`, `f2`=`model.cycle_recent`) is read from the v1.14.50 binary strings; `-m/--model` is confirmed in `opencode run --help`; daemon latest-wins is read from `metrics.go`. Not higher only because the unblocked recording (Aâ†’B on one session) has not yet been captured â€” that is the deferred tooling task.
caveats: Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode v1.14.50 supports model switching (/models, <leader>m=ctrl+x m, f2 cycle, and headless `opencode run -m`) and the daemon reads per-turn model from the SQLite store (latest-wins). The blocker is purely the driver's missing per-step model-selection primitive (gap:model-select).

The new live-TUI tmux path can OPEN the model picker (slash step types /models + Enter) but cannot COMPLETE the selection: /models is the DialogSelectModel arrow-key overlay and the live path has no `keys` step to navigate it. The headless `-m/--model` flag is the simpler, deterministic unblock and needs no overlay navigation.

recipe-lint.sh does NOT catch this gap: it is not a missing step TYPE (a recipe would use only send/wait_turn/sleep, all handled) but a missing model-selection CAPABILITY within the send arm â€” confirmed by live behavioral probing (inline /model is a no-op on headless `opencode run`).

sources.note: headless path (run_send) handles send|wait_turn|sleep and hardcodes `opencode run --format default [--session] -- text` with NO --model; live path (run_live) handles send|wait_turn|reset_session|slash|sleep â€” the slash arm types literal+Enter, no `keys` step to navigate the model picker
exposes `-m, --model <provider/model>` â€” the flag exists on the agent, but neither driver path passes it; this is the primitive the unblock threads

model_list:"model.list" bound to <leader>m (leader=ctrl+x); /models = "see and switch between available AI models" opens DialogSelectModel popover (registers keydown handlers = arrow-key overlay); model_cycle_recent bound to f2 / shift+f2; model_cycle_favorite = none

resulting session message still carried model.modelID=gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored / providerID=lmstudio â€” inline /model is NOT processed by headless `opencode run`; goes to the LLM as text and does not switch the model (rules out a false send-only path)

ComputeMetrics reads message.data.model.modelID per message and sets metrics.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(modelID) on each non-empty value (latest-non-empty-wins); TotalTokens accumulates via ev.Tokens.Total â€” a genuine A->B switch WOULD be observable (daemon_capability: full)
opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session/model state from SQLite, never tailing files â€” confirms the model is read from the store, not a JSONL trace

default model lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored; lmstudio provider declares only that one model; ionos provider declares 6 models reachable via {env:IONOS_API_TOKEN}, which is exported in ~/.zshrc (present in the recording shell) â€” model B candidate ionos/meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct
multi_model_orchestration: true (the primitive the scenario exercises)
